question_text: The predominant constituent which is responsible for strength in granite is
solution: The most common intrusive rock in Earth's continental crust, granite is familiar as a mottled pink, white, gray, and black ornamental stone. It is coarse- to medium-grained. Its three main minerals are feldspar, quartz, and mica, which occur as silvery muscovite or dark biotite or both. Of these minerals, feldspar predominates, and quartz usually accounts for more than 10 percent.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: The main ingredients of Portland cement are
solution:
Sol.(a) The main ingredients of Portland cement are lime and silica.
Ordinary Portland Cement(OPC): It is a cementing material and may be defined as a product obtained by finely pulverizing clinker produced by calcining to incipient fusion.
OPC contains two basic ingredients, namely argillaceous and calcareous. In argillaceous materials, clay predominates and in calcareous materials, calcium carbonate predominates.
Consistency of OPC cement is about 20 to 30 %
Good ordinary cement contains following ingredients.
1. Lime ($CaO$) = 60-65 %
2. silica ($SiO_2$) = 17-25%
3. Alumina ($Al_2O_3$) = 3-8 %
4. Iron Oxide ($Fe_2O_3$) = 0.5-6%
5. Magnesia ($MgO$) = 0.5-4 %
6. Sulphur = 1%
7. Alkalies:
$N_2O + K_2O$ = 0.5-1.3%
$TiO_2$ = 0.1-0.4%
$P_2O_5$ = 0.1-0.2%

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: The constituent of cement which is responsible for all the undesirable properties of cement is
solution:
Sol.(c) The constituent of cement which is responsible for all the undesirable properties of cement is tricalcium aluminate.
The compounds formed in the burning process have the properties of setting and hardening in the presence of water. They are known as Bogue compounds.
Le-Chatelier and Tornebohm have referred these compounds as Alite ($C_3S$), Belite ($C_2S$), Celite ($C_3A$) and Felite ($C_4AF$).
Tricalcium silicate ($3CaO \cdot SiO_2$): This is also known as Alite.
It is supposed to be the best cementing material and is well burnt cement. It is about 25-50% (normally about 40 per cent) of cement.
The hydrolysis of $C_3S$ is mainly responsible for 7 day strength and hardness.
Rapid hardening cement has more then 50% of $C_3S$ contant.
Dicalcium silicate ($2CaO \cdot SiO_2$): This is also known as Belite.
It is about 25-40% (normally about 32 per cent) of cement. It hydrates and hardens slowly and takes long time to add to the strength (after a year or more).
It imparts resistance to chemical attack and sulphate attack.
Tricalcium Aluminate ($3CaO \cdot Al_2O_3$): It is also known as Celite.
It is about 5-11% (normally about 10.5 per cent) of cement. It rapidly reacts with water and is responsible for flash set of finely grounded clinker.
The rapidity of action is regulated by the addition of 2 to 3 % of the gypsum at the time of grinding cement.
Tricalcium aluminate is responsible for the initial set, high heat of hydration and has greater tendency to volume changes causing cracking.
It is responsible for all the undesirable properties of cement.
Tetracalcium alumino ferrite ($4CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 \cdot Fe_2O_3$): It is also known as Felite.
It is about 8-14% (normally about 9 per cent) of cement.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: Le Chatelier's device is used for determining the
solution:
Sol.(b) Le-Chatelier's device is used for determining the soundness of cement.
Types of test for the cement :
Consistency Test: This is a test to estimate the quantity of mixing water to form a paste of normal consistency.
Soundness test:
(i) This test is ensured by limiting the quantities of free lime and magnesia which slake slowly causing change in volume of cement.
(ii) It is a very important test to assure the quality of cement since an unsound cement produces cracks, distortion and disintegration, ultimately leading to failure.
(iii) Soundness of cement may be tested by Le-Chatelier method or by autoclave method.
Le-Chatelier method: In this test 100 g cement with 0.78 times the water required to give a paste of standard consistency is taken.
This test is used to ensured by limiting the quantities of free lime.
For OPC, RHC, LHC and PPC it is limited to 10 mm, where as for HAC and SSC it should not exceed 5 mm.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: Gypsum consists of
solution:
sol.(b) Gypsum ($CaSO_4 \cdot 2H_2O$) consist of $CaSO_4$ and $H_2O$.
Gypsum: There are two commercial varieties of crude gypsum, rock gypsum and gypsum or gypsite used for the manufacture of gypsum binding material.
These substances consist principally of a hydrous sulphate of lime ($CaSO_4 \cdot 2H_2O$) with varying percentages of silica, carbonate of lime, carbonate of magnesia, and iron oxide.
Building gypsum is an air-setting binder composed mainly of semihydrate gypsum and obtained by processing gypsum at temperatures $150^\circ\text{C}-160^\circ\text{C}$.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: With increase in moisture content, the bulking of sand

options.c: first increases to a certain maximum value and then decreases
options.d: first decreases to a certain minimum value and then increases

solution:
Sol.(c) Bulking: The increase in the volume of a given mass of fine aggregate caused by the presence of water is known as bulking.
- The water forms a film over the fine aggregate particles, exerts force of surface tension and pushes them apart increasing the volume.
- The extent of bulking depends upon the percentage of moisture present in the sand and its fineness.
- With ordinary sand bulking varies from 15-30 percent. It increases with moisture content up to a certain point (4-6%), reaches maximum and then it starts decreasing.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: Which of the following cements is suitable for use in massive concrete structures such as large dams?
solution:
Sol.(b) Low heat cement(IS-12600): To limit the heat of hydration of low heat Portland cement (LHC), the tricalcium aluminate component in cement is minimised and a high percentage of dicalcium silicate and tetracalcium alumino ferrite is added.
Initial setting time = 60 minutes
Final setting time = 600 minutes
It is most suitable for large mass concrete works such as dams, large raft foundations, etc.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: The most common admixture which is used to accelerate the initial setting of concrete is
solution:
Sol.(b) Accelerators are used to accelerate the initial setting of concrete such as calcium chloride .
Admixtures: Admixtures are the materials other than the three basic ingredients of cement concrete, cement, aggregate and water added to the concrete mix before or during mixing to improve certain of its properties in fresh or hardened state.
The properties commonly modified are rate of hydration or setting time, workability, dispersion and air entrainment.
Admixtures may be classified as:
(i) Accelerators: They normally reduce the setting time, accelerate the rate of hydration of cement and consequently the rate of gain of strength.
The examples of accelerators are alkali carbonates aluminates and silates, aluminium chloride, calcium chloride, sodium chloride, sodium and potassium hydroxides, calcium formate, formaldehyde, para formaldehyde, etc.

solution:
Sol.(b) wrought iron is the purest form of iron.
Iron: Iron is by for the most important of the metals used in engineering construction.
It is available in abundance, but does not occur freely in nature. The ores of iron are classed according to the iron mineral which is predominant.
The iron content of the main ores are as follows:
Magnetite ($Fe_3O_4$) — 70-75%,
Haematite ($Fe_2O_3$) — 70%,
Limonite ($2Fe_2O_3 \cdot 3H_2O$) — 60%, it is hydrated haematite
Iron pyrite ($FeS_2$) — 47%
Siderite ($FeCO_3$) — 40%

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: The height between two floors is 3.00 m and riser is 150 mm. Assuming two flights between the floors, the number of treads will be
solution:
Height of flight = $3\text{ m} = 300\text{ cm}$.
Height of one flight = $300 / 2 = 150\text{ cm}$.
No of treads in one flight = $150 / 15 = 10$, so no of treads in one flight = $10 - 1 = 9$.
So total no of tread in one storey = $9 \times 2 = 18$.

subject: Civil Engineering

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: Dog-legged stairs are
solution:
Stairs provide access for the various floors of the building. The stair consists of series of steps(flight) with landings at appropriate intervals. Stairways are made up of individual steps. Each step consists of a tread and a riser. The tread is the flat part that you step on. The riser is the vertical(up and down) part between each tread in the stairway.
Dog legged stair case is the most economical staircase. These stairs are arranged with two adjacent flights running parallel with a mid landing. Where space is less, dog legged staircase is generally provided resulting in economical utilization of available space. In this type of staircase landing is provided corresponding to the level at which the direction of the flight changes.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: The suitable door for entrance in an airconditioned building is a
solution:
The suitable door for entrance in an airconditioned building is a revolving door.
A revolving door typically consists of three or four doors that hang on a central shaft and rotate around a vertical axis within a cylindrical enclosure. Revolving doors are energy efficient as they (acting as an airlock) prevent drafts, thus decreasing the loss of heating or cooling for the building.
Revolving doors were designed to relieve stack effect pressure in buildings.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: Extenders are added to the paint body to
solution: Extenders increases the volume and reduce the cost of paints. Extenders, when added to a paint, amounts to increase in pigment volume concentration (P.V.C.) and thus decrease the gloss, wash ability, durability, and adhesion. So, if a pigment is costly and its covering power is high, a portion of the pigment may be economically replaced by extenders without sacrificing efficiency in covering power of pigment.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text:
Match list-I with List -II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:
List-I
A. Pigment
B. Drier
C. Thinner
D. Extender
List-II
1. Turpentine
2. Iron oxide
3. Zinc sulfate
4. Aluminiumsilicate
solution:
Component of paints:
Base: The base, usually a metallic oxide, is the principal constituent of the paint. It makes the paint film opaque and possesses binding properties which reduce the shrinkage cracks in the film on drying.
Vehicles: Also known as binder, vehicle is an oil to which the base is mixed.
Pigments: These are used to hide the surface imperfections and to impart the desired colour.
Solvents: These are the oils used to thin the paints, increase the spread, and are also known as thinners.
Driers: They also known as plasticizers, are chemicals added to paint for specific purposes, e.g., as catalyst (accelerate the drying of the vehicle).

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: Which of the following test is used to determine the rate of wear of stones
solution:
This attrition test is conducted to determine the resistance power of stone against the grinding action. This test is mainly conducted to find out the suitability of a particular action. For the attrition test, 5 kg of broken angular pieces of stones is placed in both the cylinders of Deval's attrition testing machine. The cylinders are kept in an inclined position such that their axis makes an angle of 30 degrees with the horizontal. Now, the cylinders are rotated at the rate of 30 r.p.m (Revolution per minute) for 5 Hours. Later on, these pieces are removed from the cylinders and passed through a 1.5 mm sieve. The quantity of stones pieces retained on the sieve is weighed. The Loss of weight of the sample can be calculated. The percentage of wear can be calculated as:
$$\text{Percentage of wear} = \frac{\text{Loss in weight}}{\text{Original initial weight}} \times 100$$

Which of the following pairs regarding the defects in timber are correctly matched?

3. Star shakes ...................radial splits widest at the circumference and diminishing towards the centre
4. Heat shakes...................Cracks widest at the centre and diminishing towards the outer circumference
Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
solution:
Defects in Timber: Defects occurring in timber are grouped into the following divisions:
(a) Defects due to conversion: Chip mark, Diagonal grain, Torn grain, Wane.
(b) Defects due to fungi: Blue stain, Brown rot, Dry rot, Heart rot, Sap stain, Wet rot, White rot.
(c) Defects due to insects: Beetles, Marine borers, Termites.
(d) Defects due to natural forces: Burls, Callus, Chemical stain, Coarse grain, Dead wood, Druxiness, Foxiness, Knots, Rind galls, Shakes, Twisted fibres, Upsets.

subject: Civil Engineering
question_text: What is efflorescence?

options.a: Formation of white patches on the brick surface due to insoluble salts in the brick clay
options.b: Swelling of brick due to presence of carbonaceous matter and gas
options.c: Deformation of brick due to exposure to rain
options.d: Impurities in the brick clay which show after burning.

solution: Efflorescence: It is the appearance of fluffy white patches on the surface of concrete members. It is caused by poorly washed aggregate, salty water used in making concrete the salts being leaching out to the surface by rain water afterwards. This defect can be controlled to some extent by coating the surface by a water repellent.

options.b: total amount of water added at the time of mixing
options.d: maximum size of the coarse aggregate used

solution:
For a given environment, the most significant factor that influences the total shrinkage of concrete is total amount of water added at the time of mixing.
Shrinkage: Contraction of concrete in the absence of load is known as shrinkage. It may be classified as:
(a) Plastic shrinkage: Shrinkage of concrete due to absorption of water by aggregates, evaporation of water and bleeding.
(b) Drying shrinkage: The shrinkage taking place after the concrete has set and hardened.

subject: Building Materials
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
For the manufacture of good quality bricks it is essential to
1. use a reverberatory kiln
2. blend the soil with clay or sand as deemed appropriate
3. knead the soil in a ghani
4. temper the soil in a pug mill
Of these statements
solution:
Qualities of Good Brick:
(i) Bricks should be table moulded, well burnt in kilns, copper coloured, free from cracks and with sharp and square edges.
(ii) Bricks should be uniform shape and should be of standard size.
(iii) Bricks should give clear ringing sound when struck each other.
(iv) Bricks when broken should show a bright homogeneous and compact structure free from voids.
(v) Bricks should not absorb water more than 20 percent by weight for first class bricks and 22 percent by weight for second class bricks, when soaked in cold water for a period of 24 hours.
(vi) Bricks should be sufficiently hard no impression, should be left on brick surface, when it is scratched with finger nail.
(vii) Bricks should be low thermal conductivity and they should be sound proof.
(viii) Bricks should not break when dropped flat on hard ground from a height of about one meter.
(ix) Bricks, when soaked in water for 24hours, should not show deposits of white salts when allowed to dry in shade.
(x) No brick should have crushing strength below $55\text{ kg/cm}^2$

solution: If p is the standard consistency of cement the amount of water used in conducting the initial setting time test on cement is 0.85 p.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: For good bonding in brick masonry

options.a: all bricks need not be uniform in size
options.b: bats must be used in alternate courses only
options.c: the vertical joints in alternate courses should fall in plumb
options.d: cement mortar used must have surkhi as additive

solution: For good bonding in brick masonry the vertical joints in alternate courses should fall in plumb.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: Lime mortar is generally made with
solution: Lime mortar are mixture of air hardening lime or hydraulic lime, sand and water.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: If in a concrete mix the fineness modulus of coarse aggregate is 7.6, the fineness modulus of fine aggregate is 2.8 and the economical value of the fineness modulus of combined aggregate is 6.4, then the proportion of the fine aggregate is
solution:
The proportion of fine aggregate to combined aggregate:
$= \frac{P' - P}{P - P''}$
Where $P' = 7.6$, $P = 6.4$, $P'' = 2.8$
$= \frac{7.6 - 6.4}{6.4 - 2.8} \times 100 = 33.33\%$

subject: Building Materials
question_text: Tensile strength of concrete is measured by

options.a: direct tension test in the universal testing machine
options.b: applying compressive load along the diameter of the cylinder
options.c: applying third point loading on a prism
options.d: applying tensile load along the diameter of the cylinder

solution: Flexure tensile strength test is performed to estimate the tensile load at which concrete may crack. The load in this test is applied through two similar rollers mounted at third points.

solution: The texture of sand stone is granular crystalline.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: A good brick when immersed in water bath for 24 hours should not absorb more than
solution: A good brick when immersed in water bath for 24 hours should not absorb more than 20% of its dry weight.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: For complete hydration of cement, the w/c ratio needed is
solution:
In the cement about an average 23% of water by weight of cement is required for complete hydration of Portland cement.
This water combines chemically with the cement compounds and is known as bound water. Some quantity of water, about 15 per cent by weight of cement, is required to fill the cement gel pores and is known as gel water.
Therefore, a total of 38 per cent of water by weight of cement is required to complete the chemical reaction.
The water/cement ratio less than 0.38 is very common for high strength concretes. If excess water is present, it will lead to capillary cavities.
Thus for complete hydration of cement the w/c ratio needed is more than 0.35 but less than 0.45.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is a
solution: Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC) is a high strength thermoplastic material widely used in applications, such as pipes, medical devices, wire and cable insulation. It is the world's third-most widely produced synthetic plastic polymer.

subject: Building Materials
question_text: High alumina cement is produced by fusing together a mixture of

options.d: Limestone, gypsum, bauxite, clay and chalk

solution:
This is not a type of Portland cement and is manufactured by fusing 40% bauxite, 40% lime, 15% iron oxide with a little of ferric oxide and silica, magnesia, etc. at a very high temperature.
Gypsum is not use in High alumina cement.
